title: Enable TypeScript strict mode and fix all type errors
description:
## CRITICAL — TypeScript strict mode is disabled

### What's wrong
`tsconfig.json:11` has `"strict": false`. This disables ALL type safety features:
- Allows implicit `any` types
- No strict null checks
- No strict property initialization
- Unvalidated user input flows can't be caught at compile time

This is a production blocker — type errors WILL slip through and cause runtime crashes.

### What to fix
1. Change `tsconfig.json` → `"strict": true`
2. Run `npx tsc --noEmit` to find all type errors
3. Fix each error — expect errors in:
   - Components using `any` for event handlers
   - API client response types
   - Store hydration code
   - Missing null checks on optional values
4. Focus on fixing, not suppressing with `// @ts-ignore`
5. Add `"noUncheckedIndexedAccess": true` for extra safety

### Why this matters
Strict mode catches entire categories of bugs: null pointer exceptions, type confusion, missing error handling. Every major production Next.js app uses strict mode.

### Files to modify
- `tsconfig.json` — enable strict
- Multiple component/lib files — fix resulting type errors
- Likely high-impact files: `lib/api/client.ts`, `lib/auth-context.tsx`, `hooks/use-recipes.ts`, `components/photo-node.tsx`

title: Switch auth tokens from localStorage to httpOnly secure cookies
description:
## HIGH — JWT stored in localStorage is vulnerable to XSS

### What's wrong
`lib/api/client.ts:19-34` stores the JWT in localStorage:
```typescript
const TOKEN_KEY = 'cookquest_token';
export function getToken(): string | null {
  return localStorage.getItem(TOKEN_KEY);
}
```

Any XSS vulnerability (in app code OR any dependency) gives attackers full access to the JWT token, enabling account takeover.

`lib/api/auth.ts:46-67` also stores full user profile in localStorage, exposing PII (email, username).

### What to fix

**Backend changes:**
1. Set JWT as httpOnly, secure, SameSite cookie on login/register:
```typescript
res.cookie('cookquest_token', token, {
  httpOnly: true,
  secure: process.env.NODE_ENV === 'production',
  sameSite: 'strict',
  maxAge: 7 * 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000, // 7 days
  path: '/',
});
```
2. Add `GET /api/v1/auth/me` endpoint to fetch current user from cookie
3. Clear cookie on logout: `res.clearCookie('cookquest_token')`
4. Read token from cookie in auth middleware instead of Authorization header (support both during migration)

**Frontend changes:**
1. Remove `localStorage.setItem(TOKEN_KEY, token)` from auth.ts
2. Remove `getToken()`/`setToken()` functions
3. Add `credentials: 'include'` to all fetch calls in `lib/api/client.ts`
4. Only store non-sensitive data in localStorage (user ID for hydration hint)
5. Fetch full user profile from `/api/v1/auth/me` on app load

### Files to modify
- `backend/node-services/api-server/src/routes/auth.ts` — set cookies instead of returning token in body
- `backend/node-services/api-server/src/middleware/auth.ts` — read from cookie OR header
- `lib/api/client.ts` — add `credentials: 'include'`, remove localStorage token logic
- `lib/api/auth.ts` — remove localStorage user caching, add /auth/me fetch
- `lib/auth-context.tsx` — update to use cookie-based auth flow

title: Fix broken recipe images on dashboard — hero images show gray/broken alt text
description: ## HIGH — Recipe images broken across the app ### What's wrong (from Playwright screenshots) 1. **Dashboard recipe cards** — 'Perfect Boiled Egg' card shows broken image icon with alt text `Perfect Boiled Egg` visible as text. The image URL is failing to load from Unsplash. 2. **Recipe detail page** — Hero section shows a GRAY BOX (~300px tall) with no image. Only the title text overlay is visible. This is the first thing users see — looks broken/unprofessional. 3. **All recipe cards** have small circular emoji overlays (top-left) that appear but the main card images are inconsistent — some load, some don't. ### Root cause investigation - Check if Unsplash image URLs in seed data are still valid - Check if `next.config.ts` image remotePatterns are correct - Check if images use Next.js `` component with proper error fallbacks - Check `?w=800&fit=crop&q=80` query params work with current Unsplash URLs ### What to fix 1. Verify/update all Unsplash image URLs in `backend/shared/seed-data-pg.sql` 2. Add proper error fallback UI in recipe cards — gradient background with large emoji, not broken image icon 3. Add `onError` handler to all `` and `` tags: ```tsx { e.currentTarget.style.display = 'none'; // Show fallback gradient + emoji }} /> ``` 4. Recipe detail hero should use gradient fallback matching skill color, not gray box 5. Consider downloading and self-hosting recipe images for reliability ### Files to modify - `backend/shared/seed-data-pg.sql` — verify/update image URLs - `components/recipe-card.tsx` — add image error fallback - `app/recipe/[recipeId]/page.tsx` — fix hero image fallback - `next.config.ts` — verify remotePatterns configuration

title: [Phase 1] Add xp_reward column to recipes table and update seed data
description:
## CRITICAL — Recipes have no XP reward value defined

### What's wrong
The `recipes` table in `backend/shared/schema-pg.sql` has no `xp_reward` column. The core loop requires every recipe to grant XP on completion (e.g., 100 XP for beginner, 150 for intermediate, 200 for advanced). Without this, we cannot show "+100 XP" on recipe cards or in the completion toast.

### What to fix
1. **Add migration `005_add_xp_reward.sql`** to add `xp_reward INTEGER DEFAULT 100` column to `recipes` table
2. **Update `backend/shared/schema-pg.sql`** with the new column for fresh installs
3. **Update `backend/shared/seed-data-pg.sql`** to set XP values per recipe:
   - beginner recipes: 100 XP (boiled-egg, make-rice, chop-onion)
   - intermediate recipes: 150 XP (sear-steak, simmer-soup, etc.)
   - advanced recipes: 200 XP
4. **Update `backend/node-services/api-server/src/services/database.ts`** to run the new migration
5. **Update recipe API responses** to include `xp_reward` in the JSON payload
6. **Update `lib/types.ts`** to add `xpReward: number` to the Recipe interface
7. **Update `hooks/use-recipes.ts` `mapRecipe()`** to map `xp_reward` → `xpReward`

### Acceptance criteria
- `GET /api/v1/recipes` returns `xp_reward` for each recipe
- `GET /api/v1/recipes/:id` returns `xp_reward`
- Frontend Recipe type includes `xpReward`
- Migration is idempotent (can run multiple times safely)

### Files to create/modify
- `backend/shared/schema-pg.sql` — add column
- `backend/shared/seed-data-pg.sql` — add XP values
- `backend/node-services/api-server/src/services/database.ts` — add migration 005
- `lib/types.ts` — add xpReward to Recipe interface
- `hooks/use-recipes.ts` — update mapRecipe()

title: [Phase 1] Create unified POST /recipes/:id/complete endpoint
description:
## CRITICAL — No single endpoint to complete a recipe and return all updated state

### What's wrong
Currently recipe completion is handled entirely in the frontend Zustand store (localStorage). The backend has `POST /recipes/:id/progress` but it only updates a single row — it doesn't:
- Calculate XP gained
- Update streak
- Return skill progress
- Detect skill unlocks
- Detect level-ups

The core loop requires ONE endpoint that does everything and returns a rich payload.

### What to fix
Create `POST /api/v1/recipes/:id/complete` that:

1. **Marks recipe as completed** in `user_recipe_progress` (idempotent — if already completed, return current state with `already_completed: true`)
2. **Calculates XP gained** from recipe's `xp_reward` value
3. **Updates user's total XP** (add `current_xp` and `total_recipes_completed` columns to users table if not present)
4. **Calculates streak**: query `user_recipe_progress` for the user, check if they completed a recipe yesterday (streak continues) or today (already counted). Update `streak_days` and `last_activity_date` on users table.
5. **Computes skill progress**: count completed recipes in the recipe's skill
6. **Detects skill unlocks**: if skill is `basic-cooking` and completed_count == total (3), flag `skills_unlocked: ['heat-control', 'flavor-building', 'air-fryer', 'indian-cuisine']`
7. **Detects level-up**: if `current_xp >= xp_to_next_level`, increment level
8. **Returns unified payload**:
```json
{
  "success": true,
  "data": {
    "recipe_id": "boiled-egg",
    "already_completed": false,
    "xp_gained": 100,
    "user": {
      "level": 1,
      "current_xp": 100,
      "xp_to_next_level": 1000,
      "streak_days": 4,
      "total_recipes_completed": 1
    },
    "skill_progress": {
      "skill_id": "basic-cooking",
      "completed": 1,
      "total": 3,
      "percentage": 33
    },
    "level_up": null,
    "skills_unlocked": [],
    "streak_updated": true
  }
}
```

### Subtasks
- Add `current_xp`, `streak_days`, `last_activity_date`, `total_recipes_completed` columns to `users` table (migration 006)
- Create the route handler in a new file or extend `backend/node-services/api-server/src/routes/progress.ts`
- Use a database transaction to ensure atomicity
- Add authentication middleware (require login)
- Handle edge cases: recipe doesn't exist (404), user not found, already completed

### Depends on
- task_051 (xp_reward column must exist)

### Files to create/modify
- `backend/node-services/api-server/src/routes/progress.ts` — add complete endpoint
- `backend/node-services/api-server/src/services/database.ts` — add migration 006, helper queries
- `backend/node-services/api-server/src/index.ts` — register route if not already
- `backend/shared/schema-pg.sql` — add user XP/streak columns

title: [Phase 1] Implement real streak calculation from completion dates
description:
## CRITICAL — Streak is hardcoded to 3 days

### What's wrong
`app/page.tsx:34` has:
```typescript
const streak = 3 // TODO: Calculate actual streak from completion dates
```
This means every user always sees 'Streak: 3 days' regardless of activity.

### What to fix

**Option A (Server-side, preferred for logged-in users):**
The `POST /recipes/:id/complete` endpoint (task_052) already calculates streak. Create a `GET /api/v1/users/me/stats` endpoint that returns:
```json
{
  "level": 1,
  "current_xp": 0,
  "xp_to_next_level": 1000,
  "streak_days": 0,
  "total_recipes_completed": 0,
  "last_activity_date": null
}
```

**Option B (Client-side, for anonymous users):**
Calculate streak from the Zustand store. Add `completionDates` to the store:
```typescript
// In recipe-store.ts
completionDates: Map<string, string>, // recipeId → ISO date string

toggleRecipeCompletion: (recipeId) => {
  // ... existing logic ...
  // Also track: completionDates.set(recipeId, new Date().toISOString())
}

getStreak: () => {
  const dates = [...get().completionDates.values()]
    .map(d => new Date(d).toDateString())
  const uniqueDays = [...new Set(dates)].sort().reverse()
  // Walk backwards from today, counting consecutive days
  let streak = 0
  const today = new Date()
  for (let i = 0; i < uniqueDays.length; i++) {
    const expected = new Date(today)
    expected.setDate(today.getDate() - i)
    if (uniqueDays[i] === expected.toDateString()) streak++
    else break
  }
  return streak
}
```

**Implementation**: Use Option B for anonymous users + Option A for logged-in users. The dashboard should prefer server data when available.

### Subtasks
1. Add `completionDates` to Zustand store with proper serialization
2. Add `getStreak()` method to store
3. Create `GET /api/v1/users/me/stats` backend endpoint
4. Create `useUserStats()` TanStack Query hook
5. Update `app/page.tsx` to use real streak (server if logged in, store if anonymous)

### Depends on
- task_052 (unified complete endpoint handles streak server-side)

### Files to modify
- `lib/stores/recipe-store.ts` — add completionDates, getStreak()
- `app/page.tsx` — replace hardcoded streak
- `hooks/use-recipes.ts` — add useUserStats() hook
- `backend/node-services/api-server/src/routes/progress.ts` — add /users/me/stats endpoint

title: [Phase 1] Create CoreLoopHeroCard component with Start/Continue CTA
description:
## CRITICAL — New users have no clear 'Start here' guidance

### What's wrong
The dashboard shows 5 skill cards and 3 recipe cards but no prominent CTA telling users what to do next. The bottom 'Ready for your next challenge?' card is too generic and buried below the fold.

### What to build
Create `components/core-loop-hero-card.tsx` — a prominent card placed **immediately below the Level/Streak/XP header** and **above the Cooking Skills section**.

**States:**

1. **New user (0 recipes completed overall)**:
   - Title: "Start with Basic Cooking"
   - Subtitle: "Complete 3 quick recipes to unlock more skills."
   - Button: "Start Basic Cooking" → navigates to `/recipe/boiled-egg` (first Basic Cooking recipe)
   - Visual: warm gradient background (amber → orange), chef hat icon

2. **Returning user (incomplete Basic Cooking, 1-2 of 3 done)**:
   - Title: "Continue Basic Cooking"
   - Subtitle: "You've completed X of 3 recipes."
   - Button: "Resume last recipe" → navigates to the next incomplete Basic Cooking recipe
   - Progress bar showing X/3
   - Visual: same gradient, progress indicator

3. **Basic Cooking complete (3/3), other skills available**:
   - Title: "New skills unlocked!"
   - Subtitle: "Explore Heat Control, Flavor Building, and more."
   - Button: "Explore Heat Control" (primary), "Browse all skills" (secondary)
   - Visual: celebration gradient (green → emerald)

4. **All recipes completed (hide card or show mastery)**:
   - Don't render the card

### Props interface
```typescript
interface CoreLoopHeroCardProps {
  totalCompleted: number;
  basicCookingProgress: { completed: number; total: number; percentage: number };
  nextRecipeId: string | null; // ID of next incomplete recipe in Basic Cooking
  hasUnlockedSkills: boolean; // true if Basic Cooking is 100%
}
```

### Subtasks
1. Create the component with all 4 states
2. Add helper function to determine `nextRecipeId` (find first incomplete Basic Cooking recipe)
3. Integrate into `app/page.tsx` between the header Card and the grid
4. Add entrance animation (slide-up with delay)
5. Mobile responsive (full-width, stacked layout)

### Acceptance criteria
- Given a new user with 0 completed recipes, when they load the dashboard, then they see "Start with Basic Cooking" with a prominent orange CTA button
- Given a user with 1/3 Basic Cooking done, when they load the dashboard, then they see "Continue Basic Cooking" with progress 1/3 and button linking to next incomplete recipe
- Given a user with 3/3 Basic Cooking done, then they see "New skills unlocked!" with link to Heat Control
- Card is responsive (looks good on 390px mobile and 1440px desktop)
- Card has smooth entrance animation

### Files to create/modify
- `components/core-loop-hero-card.tsx` — NEW component
- `app/page.tsx` — integrate between header and grid

title: [Phase 1] Create CompletionToast component with XP, streak, and skill progress feedback
description:
## HIGH — Users get no reward feedback on recipe completion

### What's wrong
Currently `app/recipe/[recipeId]/page.tsx:30-35` shows a basic toast:
```typescript
toast.success(isCompleted ? 'marked as incomplete' : '🎉 completed!')
```
This gives zero information about XP earned, streak impact, or skill progress. The core loop requires instant, satisfying feedback.

### What to build
Create `components/completion-toast.tsx` — a rich toast/notification that appears when a recipe is marked as completed.

**Content (all in one toast, stacked vertically):**
1. "Nice work! +{xp} XP" (e.g., +100 XP) — with XP icon
2. "Basic Cooking: {x}/3 recipes completed" — with progress bar
3. "Streak: Day {n}!" (ONLY if this is the first recipe completed today) — with flame icon
4. If skill just completed (3/3): "You've mastered Basic Cooking!" bonus line
5. If level-up: "Level up! You're now Level {n}!" bonus line

**Design:**
- Use sonner custom toast (already in project) with a custom React component
- Warm background (orange/amber gradient or white with orange accent)
- Auto-dismiss after 5 seconds
- Celebratory emoji/icon
- Slide-in from top-right (default sonner behavior)

### Props interface
```typescript
interface CompletionToastProps {
  recipeName: string;
  xpGained: number;
  skillProgress: { skillName: string; completed: number; total: number };
  streakUpdated: boolean;
  streakDays: number;
  levelUp: { newLevel: number } | null;
  skillMastered: boolean;
}
```

### Integration
- Replace the simple `toast.success()` in `app/recipe/[recipeId]/page.tsx`
- Also integrate into `components/recipe-card.tsx` completion toggle
- For anonymous users: compute values from Zustand store
- For logged-in users: use response from `POST /recipes/:id/complete`

### Acceptance criteria
- Given a user completes 'Perfect Boiled Egg', then a rich toast appears showing "+100 XP", "Basic Cooking: 1/3", and streak info
- Toast auto-dismisses after 5 seconds
- Toast is beautiful and on-brand (warm colors, emojis)
- If user uncompletes a recipe, show a simple "Recipe marked as incomplete" (no XP toast)

### Depends on
- task_051 (xp_reward on recipes)

### Files to create/modify
- `components/completion-toast.tsx` — NEW component
- `app/recipe/[recipeId]/page.tsx` — replace toast.success()
- `components/recipe-card.tsx` — update completion handler

title: [Phase 1] Update RecipeCard to show XP reward badge and improved completed state
description:
## HIGH — Recipe cards don't show XP reward or clear completed state

### What's wrong
`components/recipe-card.tsx` shows recipe image, title, time, difficulty, and a small completion toggle (circle icon top-right). But:
1. No XP reward displayed (e.g., "+100 XP" badge)
2. Completed state is just a green circle — not visually satisfying
3. No disabled/already-completed button state

### What to fix

1. **Add XP badge**: Show a small badge on the card (e.g., bottom-right or near the title) with "+{xp} XP" in orange text. Only show if recipe has `xpReward` property.

2. **Improve completed state**:
   - Show a green checkmark overlay on the card image
   - Change completion button text to "Completed ✓" (not just a circle icon)
   - Add subtle green border/glow to completed cards
   - Disable the completion button (or show "Already completed" tooltip) — but still allow uncomplete on click

3. **Show skill association** more prominently if not already visible

### Design guidance
- XP badge: small pill/chip, `bg-orange-100 text-orange-700 text-xs font-semibold px-2 py-0.5 rounded-full`
- Completed overlay: `bg-green-500/20` with centered `CheckCircle2` icon
- Completed card border: `ring-2 ring-green-400`

### Acceptance criteria
- Given a recipe card for 'Perfect Boiled Egg' (beginner, 100 XP), then the card shows "+100 XP" badge
- Given a completed recipe, the card has a green overlay, checkmark, and completed text
- Given an incomplete recipe, the card shows the standard completion button
- XP badge is visible on both compact and full card layouts

### Depends on
- task_051 (xpReward in Recipe type)

### Files to modify
- `components/recipe-card.tsx` — add XP badge, improve completed state

title: [Phase 1] Create getUserCoreLoopState hook for determining next user action
[Phase 1] Wire the full completion flow: recipe complete → API → toast → real-time UI updates
description:
## HIGH — Completion flow doesn't update XP bar, recipe count, or skill progress in real-time

### What's wrong
When a user clicks 'Mark as completed':
1. Zustand store toggles the recipe in a Set (localStorage only)
2. A basic toast appears
3. The XP bar, recipe count, and skill progress DO update reactively (Zustand re-renders) but with NO animation
4. No server call is made (progress is ephemeral)

### What to fix
Wire the complete flow end-to-end:

**For logged-in users:**
1. User clicks 'Mark as completed'
2. Call `POST /api/v1/recipes/:id/complete` (from task_052)
3. On success response, update Zustand store AND show CompletionToast (task_055)
4. Animate XP bar fill from old value to new value
5. Update recipe count in header (e.g., '1 of 28 recipes completed')
6. If `skills_unlocked` in response, trigger SkillUnlockBanner (task_059)
7. If `level_up` in response, trigger level-up celebration (task_061)

**For anonymous users (no auth):**
1. User clicks 'Mark as completed'
2. Update Zustand store (existing behavior)
3. Calculate XP/streak/progress from store
4. Show CompletionToast with computed values
5. Animate XP bar

### Implementation
Create a `useCompleteRecipe()` mutation hook in `hooks/use-recipes.ts`:
```typescript
export function useCompleteRecipe() {
  const store = useRecipeStore()
  const { user } = useAuth() // check if logged in
  
  return useMutation({
    mutationFn: async (recipeId: string) => {
      if (user) {
        // Server call
        return await completeRecipe(recipeId)
      } else {
        // Local-only
        store.toggleRecipeCompletion(recipeId)
        return computeLocalCompletionResult(recipeId, store)
      }
    },
    onSuccess: (data) => {
      // Show CompletionToast
      // Invalidate queries
      // Trigger unlock/level-up if applicable
    }
  })
}
```

### Subtasks
1. Create `useCompleteRecipe()` mutation hook
2. Create `lib/api/progress.ts` with `completeRecipe(recipeId)` API call
3. Update `app/recipe/[recipeId]/page.tsx` to use the new hook
4. Update `components/recipe-card.tsx` to use the new hook
5. Add XP bar animation (CSS transition on Progress component value)
6. Add optimistic update for instant feedback

### Depends on
- task_052 (backend endpoint)
- task_055 (CompletionToast component)
- task_057 (core loop state hook)

### Acceptance criteria
- Given a logged-in user completing a recipe, the server is called and toast shows server-returned XP/streak/progress
- Given an anonymous user completing a recipe, the local store is updated and toast shows computed values
- XP bar animates smoothly from old to new value
- Recipe count updates in real-time in the header
- Skill progress updates in real-time on skill cards

### Files to create/modify
- `hooks/use-recipes.ts` — add useCompleteRecipe()
- `lib/api/progress.ts` — NEW file with completeRecipe() API call
- `app/recipe/[recipeId]/page.tsx` — use new hook
- `components/recipe-card.tsx` — use new hook

title: [Phase 2] Create SkillUnlockBanner component for Basic Cooking mastery
description:
## HIGH — No celebration when users unlock new skills

### What's wrong
When Basic Cooking reaches 3/3, skills silently become clickable. There's a 'Trophy Skill Mastered!' card at the bottom of the dashboard (page.tsx:218-230) but it's:
1. At the very bottom — users won't see it
2. Static — no animation or celebration
3. Doesn't name the newly unlocked skills
4. Doesn't direct users to try them

### What to build
Create `components/skill-unlock-banner.tsx` — a full-width celebration banner that appears when Basic Cooking goes from <3 to 3 completed recipes.

**Trigger:** When the completion flow (task_058) detects `skills_unlocked` is non-empty, OR when the Zustand store's basic-cooking progress reaches 100% for the first time in this session.

**Design:**
- Full-width banner, overlaid or inserted at top of dashboard
- Confetti animation (use CSS or a lightweight library like `canvas-confetti`)
- Title: "You've mastered Basic Cooking!" with trophy emoji
- Subtitle: "You've unlocked new skills: Heat Control, Flavor Building, Air Fryer Mastery, Indian Cuisine."
- Two buttons:
  - Primary: "Explore Heat Control" → `/skill/heat-control`
  - Secondary: "Browse all skills" → scroll to skills section
- Auto-dismiss after 10 seconds OR on button click
- Store dismissal in sessionStorage so it doesn't re-show on page reload

### Props interface
```typescript
interface SkillUnlockBannerProps {
  masteredSkillName: string;
  unlockedSkills: Array<{ id: string; name: string }>;
  onDismiss: () => void;
  onExploreSkill: (skillId: string) => void;
}
```

### Acceptance criteria
- Given a user who just completed the 3rd Basic Cooking recipe, when the dashboard renders, then a celebration banner appears with confetti
- Banner names all 4 newly unlocked skills
- Primary CTA navigates to Heat Control skill page
- Banner can be dismissed and doesn't re-appear on reload
- Banner is responsive (stacked layout on mobile)
- Animation is smooth and doesn't jank

### Depends on
- task_058 (completion flow that detects unlocks)

### Files to create/modify
- `components/skill-unlock-banner.tsx` — NEW component
- `app/page.tsx` — integrate banner trigger logic

title: [Phase 2] Implement skill card unlock state transitions with 'New!' badges
description:
## HIGH — Locked skills transition silently when unlocked

### What's wrong
`components/skill-card.tsx` handles locked state well (grayed out, 'Locked' badge, 'Complete Basic Cooking to unlock this skill' message). But when Basic Cooking is completed:
1. Skills just become clickable — no 'New!' badge
2. No visual transition or animation
3. No 'just unlocked' state to draw attention
4. Per-skill progress label missing (e.g., '0 / 5 recipes completed' for Heat Control)

### What to fix

1. **Add 'New!' badge** to freshly unlocked skills:
   - Track `newlyUnlockedSkills` in sessionStorage (set when Basic Cooking completes, cleared after first visit)
   - Show pulsing green 'New!' badge on skill cards that are in the newly-unlocked list
   - Badge: `bg-green-500 text-white text-xs font-bold px-2 py-0.5 rounded-full animate-pulse`

2. **Add entrance animation** for unlocked skills:
   - When a skill transitions from locked → unlocked, use a scale-up + glow animation
   - CSS: `@keyframes unlock { 0% { transform: scale(0.95); opacity: 0.5 } 100% { transform: scale(1); opacity: 1 } }`

3. **Add progress label per skill**:
   - Below the progress bar: "0 / 5 recipes completed" (not just "0/5 completed")
   - This already partially exists but should be more prominent

4. **Update skill card colors** when unlocked:
   - Remove gray overlay
   - Show skill's native color border/accent
   - Show 'Start Learning' and 'View recipes' links (same as Basic Cooking)

### Acceptance criteria
- Given a user who just unlocked skills, when they see the dashboard, then Heat Control, Flavor Building, Air Fryer, and Indian Cuisine show 'New!' badges
- Badges pulse gently to attract attention
- After user clicks on a newly unlocked skill, the 'New!' badge disappears on next dashboard visit
- Each skill shows 'X / Y recipes completed' progress text
- Unlocked skills show their native color accent (not gray)

### Depends on
- task_059 (skill unlock detection)

### Files to modify
- `components/skill-card.tsx` — add New! badge, progress text, unlock animation
- `app/page.tsx` — pass newlyUnlocked prop to SkillCard

title: [Phase 2] Create level-up celebration modal when XP threshold is crossed
description:
## MEDIUM-HIGH — No celebration when user levels up

### What's wrong
The header shows 'Level 1 — Beginner' and '0/1000 XP', but when the XP bar fills to 1000 and the user reaches Level 2, nothing happens. The level number just increments silently.

### What to build
Create `components/level-up-modal.tsx` — a modal/dialog that appears when a level-up is detected.

**Design:**
- Center-screen modal with overlay
- Large level number with scale-up animation (e.g., '2' zooming in)
- Title: "Level Up!"
- Subtitle: "You're now Level 2 — Beginner" (or Intermediate at level 4+)
- Show new XP threshold: "Next level: 0 / 2000 XP"
- Celebratory animation (confetti or sparkle effect)
- Single button: "Keep Cooking!" → dismisses modal
- Optional: show total recipes completed, total XP earned as stats

### Trigger
When `POST /recipes/:id/complete` returns `level_up: { newLevel: 2 }`, or when client-side calculation detects level change after recipe completion.

**Level thresholds** (from `lib/utils.ts` `calculateLevel()`):
- Level 1: 0 XP
- Level 2: 1000 XP
- Level 3: 2500 XP
- etc.

### Implementation
- Use shadcn/ui `Dialog` component for the modal
- Trigger from the completion flow (task_058)
- Only show once per level-up (use state flag)

### Acceptance criteria
- Given a user at 900 XP who completes a 100 XP recipe, when the completion response returns, then a level-up modal appears showing 'Level 2'
- Modal has animation and is visually celebratory
- Modal dismisses on button click
- Does not re-appear after dismissal

### Depends on
- task_058 (completion flow detects level-up)

### Files to create/modify
- `components/level-up-modal.tsx` — NEW component
- `app/page.tsx` or completion flow — trigger modal

title: [Phase 2] Add skill unlock computation to backend progress service
description:
## HIGH — Backend doesn't know about skill dependencies or unlock logic

### What's wrong
The frontend currently handles skill locking/unlocking in `app/page.tsx:120-121`:
```typescript
const isLocked = skill.id !== 'basic-cooking' && basicCookingProgress.percentage < 100
```
This is client-only logic. The backend has no concept of:
- Which skills depend on which other skills
- When a skill should unlock
- What the skill dependency tree looks like

For the `POST /recipes/:id/complete` endpoint to return `skills_unlocked`, the backend needs this logic.

### What to fix

1. **Add `required_skill_id` and `required_recipes_completed` columns to `skills` table:**
```sql
ALTER TABLE skills ADD COLUMN required_skill_id TEXT REFERENCES skills(id);
ALTER TABLE skills ADD COLUMN required_recipes_completed INTEGER DEFAULT 0;
```

2. **Update seed data** for skills:
```sql
-- basic-cooking: no requirements (always unlocked)
UPDATE skills SET required_skill_id = NULL, required_recipes_completed = 0 WHERE id = 'basic-cooking';
-- All others: require basic-cooking 3/3
UPDATE skills SET required_skill_id = 'basic-cooking', required_recipes_completed = 3 WHERE id IN ('heat-control', 'flavor-building', 'air-fryer', 'indian-cuisine');
```

3. **Add unlock check function in database service:**
```typescript
async getNewlyUnlockedSkills(userId: number, completedSkillId: string): Promise<Skill[]> {
  // Query skills where required_skill_id = completedSkillId
  // AND user has completed >= required_recipes_completed in that skill
  // AND skill is not already 'unlocked' for this user
}
```

4. **Integrate into complete endpoint** (task_052):
   - After marking recipe complete, check if the skill is now fully completed
   - If so, query for skills that this skill unlocks
   - Return them in `skills_unlocked` array

### Acceptance criteria
- When a user completes the 3rd Basic Cooking recipe, the complete endpoint returns `skills_unlocked: ['heat-control', 'flavor-building', 'air-fryer', 'indian-cuisine']`
- When a user completes the 1st or 2nd Basic Cooking recipe, `skills_unlocked` is empty
- Skill dependencies are stored in DB, not hardcoded in backend logic

### Depends on
- task_052 (complete endpoint structure)

### Files to modify
- `backend/shared/schema-pg.sql` — add columns to skills table
- `backend/shared/seed-data-pg.sql` — set dependency data
- `backend/node-services/api-server/src/services/database.ts` — add migration + unlock query
- `backend/node-services/api-server/src/routes/progress.ts` — integrate unlock check

title: [Phase 1] Implement idempotent recipe completion (prevent double-complete)
description:
## HIGH — Users can complete the same recipe multiple times

### What's wrong
The current Zustand store's `toggleRecipeCompletion` is a toggle — it adds or removes from a Set. This means:
1. Clicking 'Mark as completed' twice uncompletes the recipe (confusing UX)
2. There's no guard against accidental double-clicks
3. The backend endpoint (when implemented) could award duplicate XP

### What to fix

1. **Frontend: Separate complete and uncomplete actions**
   - `completeRecipe(recipeId)` — only adds, never removes. Shows error toast if already completed.
   - `uncompleteRecipe(recipeId)` — explicit uncomplete with confirmation dialog
   - Keep `toggleRecipeCompletion` for backward compatibility but prefer explicit methods

2. **Frontend: Debounce/disable button after click**
   - After clicking 'Mark as completed', disable the button for 2 seconds
   - Show loading spinner during API call
   - On success, change button to 'Completed ✓' (non-clickable unless long-press/click reveals 'Undo')

3. **Backend: Idempotent endpoint**
   - `POST /recipes/:id/complete` should check if already completed
   - If already completed: return `{ already_completed: true, ... }` with current state but NO additional XP
   - Use UPSERT: `INSERT INTO user_recipe_progress ... ON CONFLICT (user_id, recipe_id) DO NOTHING`

4. **Show 'Already completed' state**
   - If user tries to complete again: toast saying 'You've already completed this recipe!'
   - Completed recipes show checkmark and green state

### Acceptance criteria
- Given a user who completed 'Perfect Boiled Egg', when they click 'Mark as completed' again, then they see 'Already completed' (no double XP)
- Backend returns 200 with `already_completed: true` on duplicate completion
- Button shows loading state during API call
- Accidental double-clicks don't cause issues

### Files to modify
- `lib/stores/recipe-store.ts` — add explicit complete/uncomplete methods
- `components/recipe-card.tsx` — debounce button, loading state
- `app/recipe/[recipeId]/page.tsx` — update completion handler
- `backend/node-services/api-server/src/routes/progress.ts` — idempotent logic

title: [Phase 1] Update ProgressSummaryHeader to show real-time XP, streak, level, and recipe count
description:
## HIGH — Header progress bar and stats don't update smoothly

### What's wrong
The dashboard header in `app/page.tsx:56-95` shows:
- Level (computed from completed count)
- Streak (hardcoded to 3)
- Recipes completed count
- XP bar

But:
1. XP bar has no animation (jumps instantly)
2. Streak is hardcoded (task_053 fixes the data, this task fixes the display)
3. No visual feedback when values change
4. Level label doesn't animate on level-up

### What to fix

1. **Animate XP bar**: Add CSS transition to the Progress component value:
```css
.progress-bar-fill {
  transition: width 0.8s cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1);
}
```
The shadcn/ui Progress component uses `style={{ transform: translateX(-${100-value}%) }}`. Override or extend to add transition.

2. **Animate recipe count**: When count changes, briefly highlight the number (e.g., scale-up + color flash)

3. **Animate streak**: When streak increments, briefly show flame icon pulsing

4. **Update header to use real data**:
   - Use `useCoreLoopState()` hook (task_057) for all values
   - Display: Level N — {title}, Streak: {n} days, {completed} of {total} recipes, {xp}/{nextLevelXp} XP

5. **Mobile layout improvement**: Ensure streak flame badge shows current streak count (not hardcoded 3)

### Acceptance criteria
- XP bar smoothly animates from old value to new value over ~0.8 seconds
- Recipe count briefly highlights when incremented
- Streak displays real calculated value (not hardcoded 3)
- All values update in real-time when recipes are completed (no page reload needed)
- Mobile layout shows streak correctly

### Depends on
- task_053 (real streak data)
- task_057 (core loop state hook)

### Files to modify
- `app/page.tsx` — update header to use real data + animations
- `components/ui/progress.tsx` — add transition to progress bar fill (if needed)

title: [Phase 3] Refine all core loop microcopy for warmth and encouragement
description:
## MEDIUM — Copy is functional but not warm/encouraging (Strava + Duolingo vibe missing)

### What's wrong
Current copy is generic:
- 'Ready for your next challenge?' (bottom CTA) — too vague
- 'Mark as completed' — no excitement
- 'Recipe completed!' toast — no personality
- No encouraging microcopy throughout the journey

### What to fix
Update copy across all core loop touchpoints to be warm, encouraging, and personality-driven:

**CoreLoopHeroCard (task_054):**
- New user: "Start your cooking journey" / "Complete 3 Basic Cooking recipes to unlock more skills."
- Returning: "Welcome back, chef!" / "You're {X}/3 through Basic Cooking. Keep going!"
- Unlocked: "New skills unlocked!" / "You've opened up Heat Control, Flavor Building, Air Fryer Mastery, and Indian Cuisine."

**CompletionToast (task_055):**
- "Nice work! +100 XP earned."
- "Basic Cooking: {x}/3 recipes done."
- "Streak updated: Day {n}! Keep the fire going!"

**SkillUnlockBanner (task_059):**
- "You've mastered Basic Cooking!"
- "Your kitchen just got bigger. Explore 4 new skill tracks."

**Level-up modal (task_061):**
- "Level {n}! You're becoming a real chef."

**Recipe detail page:**
- Completed state: "Great job, chef! This one's in the bag."
- CTA: "I made this!" instead of "Mark as completed"

**Dashboard CTA (replace existing):**
- "Your kitchen awaits! Pick a skill and keep leveling up." → "Every recipe brings you closer to mastery. What's cooking today?"

### Acceptance criteria
- All user-facing strings are warm, encouraging, and on-brand
- No generic/placeholder copy remains
- Copy is consistent across components
- Microcopy drives action (not just informs)

### Files to modify
- `components/core-loop-hero-card.tsx`
- `components/completion-toast.tsx`
- `components/skill-unlock-banner.tsx`
- `components/level-up-modal.tsx`
- `app/recipe/[recipeId]/page.tsx`
- `app/page.tsx`

title: [Phase 3] Add entrance animations and micro-interactions for core loop elements
description:
## MEDIUM — Core loop elements appear without animation or delight

### What's wrong
Some animations exist (slide-up on skill cards, fade-in on recipes) but the core loop elements need more polished micro-interactions:
1. XP counter doesn't count up (just appears as final number)
2. Streak flame doesn't animate
3. Completion checkmarks don't animate
4. Unlock transitions are instant
5. Progress bars don't fill with animation

### What to fix

1. **XP counter animation**: When XP increases, count up from old value to new value over 1.5 seconds (like a slot machine). Use `requestAnimationFrame` or a lightweight counter library.

2. **Streak flame pulse**: When streak is active (>0), the flame icon gently pulses. When streak increments, burst animation (scale up + glow).

3. **Completion checkmark**: When recipe is completed, the checkmark draws itself (SVG path animation) with a satisfying green glow.

4. **Skill card unlock animation**: When a skill goes from locked → unlocked, animate:
   - Gray → color transition (0.5s)
   - Lock icon → skill icon morph
   - Subtle glow/shimmer effect

5. **Progress bar fill**: Smooth fill animation with a slight overshoot (spring physics).

6. **CoreLoopHeroCard entrance**: Slide up with spring physics, slightly delayed after header loads.

### Implementation notes
- Use CSS animations/transitions where possible (performant)
- Use `framer-motion` only if already installed (check package.json) — otherwise pure CSS
- Add `will-change` hints for animated elements
- Respect `prefers-reduced-motion` media query

### Acceptance criteria
- XP counter counts up visually when XP increases
- Flame icon pulses gently for active streaks
- Completion creates a satisfying checkmark animation
- All animations respect reduced-motion preferences
- No jank or layout shifts during animations

### Files to modify
- `app/page.tsx` — XP counter, streak animations
- `components/recipe-card.tsx` — completion animation
- `components/skill-card.tsx` — unlock animation
- `components/core-loop-hero-card.tsx` — entrance animation
- `app/globals.css` or `tailwind.config.ts` — add keyframes

title: [Phase 3] Add daily quest suggestion card ('Complete 1 recipe today')
description:
## MEDIUM-LOW — No daily engagement hook to keep users returning

### What's wrong
There's no 'daily quest' or 'daily goal' to encourage users to complete at least 1 recipe per day. Streaks exist but aren't tied to visible daily objectives.

### What to build
Create `components/daily-quest-card.tsx` — a small card shown on the dashboard when the user hasn't completed a recipe today.

**States:**
1. **Not completed today**: Show a card below the CoreLoopHeroCard:
   - Icon: target/calendar icon
   - Title: "Daily Quest"
   - Subtitle: "Complete 1 recipe today to keep your streak alive!"
   - Progress: "0/1 recipes today"
   - CTA: "Start cooking" → link to next recommended recipe

2. **Completed today**: Show completed state:
   - Icon: checkmark
   - Title: "Daily Quest Complete!"
   - Subtitle: "You've cooked today. Streak: Day {n}!"
   - Optional: "Want to do more? Try another recipe →"

3. **All recipes done**: Don't show

**Logic:**
- Check if any recipe was completed with today's date
- For anonymous users: check Zustand store completionDates
- For logged-in users: check last_activity_date from server

### Acceptance criteria
- Given a user who hasn't completed a recipe today, they see the daily quest card
- Given a user who completed 1+ recipes today, they see the completed state
- Card links to the next recommended recipe
- Card is small/compact (doesn't dominate the dashboard)

### Depends on
- task_053 (streak/completion dates in store)

### Files to create/modify
- `components/daily-quest-card.tsx` — NEW component
- `app/page.tsx` — integrate below CoreLoopHeroCard
